title: Sour Tender Syrian Lamb Kebabs
description: A traditional Syrian dish featuring ground lamb, annatto, and a blend of spices, resulting in a sour and tender flavor profile. This recipe is halal-friendly and perfect for adventurous eaters.
instructions: Preheat the grill to medium-high heat.
In a large bowl, combine the ground lamb, ground annatto, sumac, salt, and black pepper. Mix well with your hands until just combined.
Add the pomegranate molasses, lemon juice, and olive oil to the bowl. Mix until the mixture is cohesive, being careful not to overmix.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
Thread the lamb mixture onto skewers, leaving a small space between each piece.
Grill the kebabs for 10-12 minutes, turning occasionally, or until they reach an internal temperature of 145°F.
Remove the kebabs from the grill and let them rest for 5 minutes.
Serve the kebabs hot, garnished with minced garlic and a side of pita bread or salad.
To blanch the garlic, submerge it in boiling water for 10 seconds, then immediately transfer it to an ice bath to stop the cooking process.
To enhance the sour flavor, serve the kebabs with a squeeze of fresh lemon juice.
